sources: UN Meetings Coverage and Press Releases

items.headline: U.S. Supreme Court Strikes Down Medication Abortion Restriction
items.body: The U.S. Supreme Court blocked an attempt to restrict access to the abortion medication mifepristone. This action ensures patients can continue to receive the medication through telehealth and mail services.

items.headline: Secretary-General Welcomes Deal for Exchange of Conflict-Related Detainees in Yemen
items.body: Parties in the Yemen conflict agreed to release more than 1,600 detainees. The agreement allows for the imminent reunion of these individuals with their families.

items.headline: 2026 Long Island Rail Road strike
items.body: Over 3,500 Long Island Rail Road workers began a strike over pay disputes. The action suspended service for more than 300,000 daily commuters in the United States.

items.headline: U.S. Sanctions Target Cuba's Military Regime, Elites
items.body: The United States government imposed new sanctions on a Cuban military-controlled business enterprise. The action blocks the company's property in the U.S. and impacts foreign firms doing business there.

es.headline: Supreme Court restricts path to permanent jobs for public workers
es.body: The Supreme Court ruled against automatically making temporary public workers permanent. This decision creates job uncertainty for thousands of interim employees.

no.headline: Government And Farmers' Unions Finalize Agricultural Deal
no.body: The state and farmers' unions finalized a 3.66 billion kroner deal. The agreement now dictates food prices and farm incomes for a year.

nl.headline: Right-wing parties form new Dutch government coalition
nl.body: Four parties reached an agreement to form a new government. The pact sets a new course on immigration and climate policy.
